content.full_text:
August 6, 1991

Ms. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95804-0814

Dear Ms. Hughes,

This letter is to confirm our conversation of August 2, during which we discussed issues related to individuals holding concurrent positions with both controlled and independent committees. Thank you for your assistance with this matter. I have received from you two letters on the subject (To Karen Brainerd, ROAR, Dec. 27, 1990, and To Palmer Madden, October 4, 1985). Based on our conversation and review of the letters, I make the following conclusions:

1) An individual holding positions, including officerships, on both controlled and independent committees does not in itself establish control of the independent committee by a candidate, nor any violation of FPPC regulations.

2) Other considerations, including coordination of decisionmaking, developing or implementing strategy in concert, or substantial fundraising by a candidate for use by the independent committee, are the crucial factors which constitute control.

I intend to act upon these conclusions, and to advise others of them. Should you find error with the conclusions I have drawn, please contact me at (805) 963-0496. Thank you again for your help.

Sincerely,

Gregory Helms,
Executive Director
